message:
Last week my kid asked, 'Mom, where's the flashlight?' during a minor power flicker. I confidently walked to three different drawers, found dead batteries, a flashlight with no head, and what I'm pretty sure was a glow stick from 2019. We ended up using my phone light like it was 2005.

Turns out being *almost* ready is just another way of not being ready—and honestly, it's kind of funny until it's not.

Since then I actually grouped our essentials in one spot: working flashlights (batteries checked!), water, first aid, important docs in a folder. Nothing fancy. Just... actually doable.

What's your 'I thought I had this covered' preparedness moment? 😄
